scoping.selected_writer_rationale: sh.SUMMARIZE_CHANNEL_SALES is the sole direct writer to sh.CHANNEL_SALES_SUMMARY, performing a DELETE+INSERT+MERGE cycle to fully refresh the table each run.

classification.rationale: The procedure performs a full DELETE followed by INSERT...SELECT with GROUP BY aggregation over CHANNELS JOIN SALES using a CTE, then a MERGE loop to update the TIER column per channel — matching the fact_aggregate full-refresh pattern.

primary_key.rationale: CHANNEL_ID is the declared primary key (CHANNEL_SALES_SUMMARY_PK constraint) and is a natural business identifier from the CHANNELS source table; no surrogate generation occurs in the proc.

natural_key.rationale: No auto_increment column and no surrogate key generation (NEWID, SEQUENCE, etc.) in the proc body; CHANNEL_ID is the business-level channel identifier passed through from the CHANNELS table.

watermark.rationale: UPDATED_AT (DATE) matches the watermark naming pattern and is set to SYSDATE in both the INSERT and the MERGE UPDATE statements — recording the time of each full refresh.

refactor.extracted_sql:
WITH channel_totals AS (
    SELECT
        c.CHANNEL_ID,
        c.CHANNEL_DESC,
        SUM(s.AMOUNT_SOLD) AS TOTAL_AMOUNT
    FROM   SH.CHANNELS c
    JOIN   SH.SALES    s ON s.CHANNEL_ID = c.CHANNEL_ID
    GROUP BY c.CHANNEL_ID, c.CHANNEL_DESC
)
SELECT
    CHANNEL_ID,
    CHANNEL_DESC,
    TOTAL_AMOUNT,
    CASE
        WHEN TOTAL_AMOUNT >= 500000 THEN 'HIGH'
        WHEN TOTAL_AMOUNT >= 100000 THEN 'MEDIUM'
        ELSE 'LOW'
    END AS TIER,
    SYSDATE AS UPDATED_AT
FROM channel_totals
refactor.refactored_sql:
WITH channels AS (
    SELECT * FROM SH.CHANNELS
),

sales AS (
    SELECT * FROM SH.SALES
),

channel_totals AS (
    SELECT
        c.CHANNEL_ID,
        c.CHANNEL_DESC,
        SUM(s.AMOUNT_SOLD) AS TOTAL_AMOUNT
    FROM channels c
    JOIN sales s ON s.CHANNEL_ID = c.CHANNEL_ID
    GROUP BY c.CHANNEL_ID, c.CHANNEL_DESC
),

channel_tiers AS (
    SELECT
        CHANNEL_ID,
        CHANNEL_DESC,
        TOTAL_AMOUNT,
        CASE
            WHEN TOTAL_AMOUNT >= 500000 THEN 'HIGH'
            WHEN TOTAL_AMOUNT >= 100000 THEN 'MEDIUM'
            ELSE 'LOW'
        END AS TIER
    FROM channel_totals
),

final AS (
    SELECT
        CHANNEL_ID,
        CHANNEL_DESC,
        TOTAL_AMOUNT,
        TIER,
        SYSDATE AS UPDATED_AT
    FROM channel_tiers
)

SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both extracted and refactored SQL read from the same Oracle source tables: SH.CHANNELS and SH.SALES.

output_columns.summary: Both SQL forms produce CHANNEL_ID, CHANNEL_DESC, TOTAL_AMOUNT, TIER, and UPDATED_AT for SH.CHANNEL_SALES_SUMMARY.

joins.summary: Both implementations preserve the CHANNELS to SALES join on CHANNEL_ID before aggregation.

filters.summary: Neither version adds extra row filters; both apply only the channel sales aggregation and tier classification logic from the source procedure.

aggregation_grain.summary: Both operate at one row per channel after summing AMOUNT_SOLD by CHANNEL_ID and CHANNEL_DESC.
